observed.statement: input is split on every semicolon before escape interpretation

inferred.statement: escaped semicolons create malformed pair fragments

unresolved.statement: the canonical escaping rules for consecutive backslashes are not fully specified
unresolved.basis: example contract covers only representative forms

impact_map: configuration text -> lexical separation -> key/value split -> runtime configuration

invariants.statement: escape processing preserves the intended literal value

risks.statement: escaped delimiter -> premature lexical split -> malformed or incorrect configuration

scenarios.title: escaped delimiter remains literal
preconditions: value contains an escaped semicolon followed by another pair
expected: escaped semicolon remains in first value
forbidden: escaped delimiter creates a pair boundary

findings.statement: lexical splitting occurs before escape recognition and breaks escaped delimiter values
findings.next_action: tokenize with escape-aware scanning, then rerun example and grammar-boundary tests

residual_risks.statement: complete consecutive-backslash and malformed-input grammar is unspecified
residual_risks.treatment: obtain grammar authority and add properties before broad release

findings: NOT_READY is supported; broader grammar claim remains intentionally bounded

authority_required: parser owner confirms full escape grammar before generalized fix
